sourceMovie.title: Uncanny
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Uncanny (2015) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Matthew Leutwyler's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Uncanny clicked for you, The Matrix Revolutions (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Uncanny?
faq.answer: Try The Matrix Revolutions, Upgrade, I Am Mother—each shares DNA with Uncanny while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Uncanny?
faq.answer: Uncanny (2015) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Uncanny worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
